title: CCRG iter-8 Positioning: Chanin/Max-Precision Delta, Concentration-as-Sharp-Gate, Localization+Editing Retarget, Locked Cites
summary: Finalizes the iteration-8 CCRG paper positioning for GEN_PAPER_TEXT across four deliverables. (D1) The label-free DELTA of CCRG's 'anchored recall-hole-guided precision selection of a single absorber' vs (i) Chanin et al.'s SUPERVISED absorption diagnostic (2409.14507: LR probe on ground-truth first-letter labels -> max encoder-cosine parent; ablation on the first-letter logit + probe-projection test for the absorber; absorption_rate=num_absorptions/lr_probe_true_positives; demonstrated only on spelling) and vs (ii) a simple max-precision selector (empirically a TIE since every win is effectively k=1) -- with the M5''' novelty trim that demotes set-cover/(1-1/e) to MOTIVATION only. (D2) Grounds the mechanism reframe 'the edit win tracks latent CONCENTRATION/PRECISION, not absorption structure' against the feature-selection-for-steering literature: Arad/Mueller/Belinkov (EMNLP2025, output-score selection -> 2-3x), CorrSteer (correlation selection), FGAA (density filtering), Sparse Activation Steering (width->monosemanticity->sharper), Duan 2606.08365 (pre-intervention feature statistics predict collateral spread -- strongest direct support), SAE-TS (target specific feature, min side-effects), and Anthropic Scaling Monosemanticity (specificity = sharp conditional gate). Internal evidence: concentration v2=prec*max_forget_kg r=+0.63 predicts wins; absorption-label r=-0.09; footprint-sparsity v1=prec/f_kg ANTI r=-0.80. (D3) Retarget to lead with 'training-free auditable LOCALIZATION + EDITING of homograph-polysemy absorption' (ICLR primary per goal, ICML acceptable; ICLR2026 CfP lists fairness/safety/privacy + interpretation of learned representations), safety-homograph null (2/44, both homographs) as HEADLINE limitation; supplies BOTH a wins-landed (OUTCOME-A) and base-thin (OUTCOME-B, expected) abstract+intro spine with a one-line selector (parallel M1'''/M3''' = BEATS=0/FAIR_CLOSES=4/n_concentrated_wins=0 -> OUTCOME-B). (D4) Locks the full citation set: carries iter-6/iter-7 venue-verified table verbatim + 7 new web-verified concentration/precision-steering cites with IDs/venues/authors + BibTeX + unresolved-flags list. (D5) 14-item presentation-strip checklist (lead +1.00 not +1.58; both forget instruments; unify gate operator; set-cover=motivation; concentration not absorption; localization-not-classification; retract Georgia +0.561; firing-signature != edit-handle). Outputs research_out.json + research_report.md (D1-D5).
answer:
This positioning pass finalizes the iteration-8 CCRG paper framing so GEN_PAPER_TEXT can write a defensible draft regardless of how the parallel iter-8 experiments land. It resolves the three iter-7 publication-gating majors (oversold set-cover novelty; unsupported absorption-causation; n~2 positive edit base) and locks the citation set.

=== D1. Method-identity = anchored recall-hole-guided PRECISION SELECTION; delta vs Chanin and vs max-precision ===
Chanin et al. (arXiv 2409.14507, NeurIPS 2025 [12]) define the absorption diagnostic as a fully SUPERVISED, task-bound procedure [1]: (a) the parent latent is found by training a logistic-regression probe on GROUND-TRUTH first-letter labels and selecting the SAE latent 'whose encoder direction has highest cosine similarity with the probe'; (b) the absorbing latent is found by ablation on a TASK-SPECIFIC logit (the first-letter logit) plus a probe-projection test (removing the probe direction from the absorber kills its ablation effect); (c) the metric is absorption_rate = num_absorptions / lr_probe_true_positives, with thresholds cosine>0.025 and a 1.0 ablation-gap [1]. Every demonstration is the first-letter spelling task ('complete access to ground truth labels for all tokens'); the OpenReview meta-review itself flags that the metric and results 'focus on the first-letter task' and generalizability is open [1, 2]. So Chanin DETECTS/MEASURES absorption on a NAMED latent GIVEN a supervised probe and a task logit, and is form-bound to spelling; it does NOT propose the parent+absorber pair as a usable, editable handle WITHOUT supervision. CCRG's DELTA is therefore label-free, training-free, and form-free: the parent is anchored by content-response RECALL on counterfactual pairs (no probe), the recall hole is the parent's uncovered counterfactual pairs (no logit), and the precise sub-context latent is chosen by firing-PRECISION on the counterfactual partition; the form-free probe+ablation diagnostic is used ONLY to SCORE already-formed feature-KG edges (non-circular), never to FORM units. The buy is the training-free SURFACING of the precise sub-context latent that marginal-attribution selection silently drops, extended beyond spelling to named-entity homographs and co-firing toxicity. Against a simple max-precision (S-prec) selector, the open M3''' question is whether anchored recall-hole set-cover beats 'pick the single most precise latent on the target sub-context.' Because every reported edit win is effectively k=1, the empirical answer is a TIE (where set-cover absorber == max-prec latent it is inert; the one divergent case, Amazon, has a stronger forget but a discovery-equivalent preservation joint), so set-cover ADDS preservation value in 0 cases. The novelty is therefore TRIMMED: method identity = 'anchored recall-hole-guided precision selection of a single absorber'; set-cover and the (1-1/e) greedy guarantee are demoted to MOTIVATION for the disjoint-support coverage view only. What discovery buys over bare max-precision is the recall-hole ANCHORING that names WHICH sub-context to select a precise latent FOR (max-precision needs the sub-context handed to it). A defensive novelty check finds no existing named method that IS 'anchored recall-hole precision selection': FGAA selects by density [5], CorrSteer by correlation [4], Arad by output-score [3] -- all select for steering EFFECT or correlation, not for the recall hole an absorbed parent leaves; the recall-hole concept itself is Chanin/LessWrong's description of absorption [1, 13], and using it as the selection anchor is the new step.

=== D2. Mechanism = CONCENTRATION/PRECISION, not absorption (positioned, not asserted) ===
The claim to ground is that the edit advantage is a property of a PRECISE/CONCENTRATED latent acting as a SHARPER conditional gate than a footprint-matched dense projection, with absorption only ONE label-free source of concentration. Internally this is load-bearing: the win predictor is concentration v2 = precision x single-latent leverage (prec x max_forget_kg), point-biserial r=+0.63, whereas the absorption LABEL gives r=-0.09 and footprint-sparsity v1=prec/f_kg ANTI-correlates r=-0.80 (distributed Georgia/Jordan also fire sparsely, so sparse footprint != concentration); wins cross labels (absorber 'large' AND co-firing 'insult' win; absorbers Georgia/Jordan/Bush/Cook lose). The external literature independently shows that WHICH precise feature you select -- not the steering operator -- governs clean, low-collateral intervention. Arad/Mueller/Belinkov (EMNLP 2025) show SAEs become competitive with supervised steering only AFTER filtering out low-output-score features, a 2-3x improvement purely from selecting specific/effective features, and that high input- and output-scores rarely co-occur (clean handles are a minority) [3]. CorrSteer selects by correlation to 'extract more relevant features, thereby reducing spurious correlations' [4]. FGAA must FILTER OUT high-density features and keep only specialized (low-density, high-precision) ones [5]. Sparse Activation Steering finds scaling SAE width increases monosemanticity, 'better disentangling features by reducing overlap' for finer-grained, more reliable interventions [6]. Duan forecasts SAE steering side effects (collateral spread) from feature statistics computed BEFORE steering -- a direct external test of 'concentration predicts collateral' [7]. SAE-TS targets specific SAE features while minimizing unintended side effects [8]. Anthropic's Scaling Monosemanticity operationalizes specificity as 'when the feature is active, the relevant concept is reliably present,' so a precise feature's firing IS a sharp conditional signal [9]. Putting these together: a concentrated latent's calibrated JumpReLU firing is itself a sharp, threshold-free conditional gate, whereas a footprint-matched DENSE projection must be driven to beta~3 over-erasure to match the same forget (~13.8x the collateral of its own ungated edit on 'large'). So the edit advantage is sharper conditioning from a concentrated detector, and the paper must DROP 'the win traces to the absorption structure' in favor of 'the win tracks concentration; absorption is one label-free route to a concentrated handle but neither necessary (insult co-fires yet wins) nor sufficient (Georgia/Jordan absorb yet lose).' The gate MECHANISM is prior art and supervised (locked CAST/GSS/GUARD-IT/SADI from iter-7); the SAE-specific value is the label-free discovery of WHERE to gate.

=== D3. Retarget to LOCALIZATION + EDITING; venue fit; both spines ===
Lead with 'training-free, auditable LOCALIZATION (+ EDITING on concentrated features) of homograph-polysemy absorption,' with the safety-homograph-confinement null (2/44 attributes, both homographs; 0/28 professions; 3/64 homograph entities) as the HEADLINE limitation, the concentrated-feature edit (large +1.00 CI[0.79,1.21] over the strongest ungated dense; Amazon +0.75) as a SCOPED capability bounded by the genuinely-fair bounded-beta d_sub-gated control, classification SUPPORTING/within-SAE, router DEMOTED. The GOAL is authoritative: ICLR primary, ICML acceptable. ICLR 2026's CfP lists 'societal considerations including fairness, safety, privacy' and 'visualization or interpretation of learned representations' [10], a clean fit for the localization + editable-feature-KG + label-free-discovery framing; ICML 2026's main full-paper deadline was Jan 28 2026, so the live cycle for a mid-2026 paper is ICLR 2026, with ICML an acceptable fallback on AREA grounds [11]. The contribution maps to the goal's reviewer-evaluable areas (clustering, feature selection, classification, knowledge graphs/extraction, applied knowledge discovery, text data analytics, LLMs/deep learning) far better than a classification-win framing, because the paper makes NO out-classifies-dense claim (consistent with the broader finding that simple dense baselines often beat raw-latent SAE steering [3, 15]). TWO spines are supplied: OUTCOME-A (>=4 independent concentrated wins land under the fair control) foregrounds the editable capability led by +1.00 (never +1.58); OUTCOME-B (base stays thin n~2 OR fair-gated matches KG) leads fully with the auditability/localization spine and the homograph-confinement null, with the contribution = label-free DISCOVERY of where to gate. Selector: use OUTCOME-A only if >=4 concentrated wins land under the fair bounded-beta d_sub-gated control, else OUTCOME-B. Per the parallel M1'''/M3''' analysis (BEATS=0, FAIR_CLOSES=4, NO_MEANINGFUL=4, n_concentrated_wins=0), OUTCOME-B is the expected selection.

=== D4/D5. Cites locked + presentation strip ===
The full iter-6/iter-7 venue-verified table is carried verbatim (Chanin 2409.14507 NeurIPS2025; AxBench/SAEBench/Matryoshka ICML2025; SPLINCE/Karvonen-Marks NeurIPS2025; Ahsan-Wallace ICLR2026; CAST ICLR2025-Spotlight; GSS/GUARD-IT/DAC preprints; Peng 2506.23845; the iter-6 debiasing + reweighting + set-cover + cross-field grouping sets). SEVEN new concentration/precision-steering cites were web-verified this iteration: Arad-Mueller-Belinkov 2505.20063 (EMNLP 2025 main, DOI 10.18653/v1/2025.emnlp-main.519) [3]; CorrSteer 2508.12535 Cho/Wu/Koshiyama (arXiv Comments claim ICML 2026 but also listed on iclr.cc/virtual/2026 -> cite as PREPRINT + FLAG) [4, 14]; FGAA 2501.09929 Soo et al. (preprint) [5]; Sparse Activation Steering 2503.00177 Bayat/Rahimi-Kalahroudi/Pezeshki/Chandar/Vincent (preprint) [6]; Duan 2606.08365 (preprint, strongest direct support) [7]; SAE-TS 2411.02193 Chalnev/Siu/Conmy (preprint, supporting) [8]; Anthropic Scaling Monosemanticity (Templeton et al. 2024, cite by URL) [9]. BibTeX is emitted for all seven; the unresolved-flags list covers the CorrSteer venue conflict, Karvonen-Marks workshop, DeBiasLens CVPR2026, Deng dual-listing, GUARD-IT/GSS/DAC preprint venues, and SAUCE withdrawal. The 14-item presentation-strip checklist directs: lead +1.00 (caveat +1.58 with the beta~2.97 / 0.290-vs-0.021 over-erasure note); report BOTH forget instruments and match on a behavioral measure (they sign-disagree at the KL-matched point on 'large'); unify the gate operator; set-cover=motivation; mechanism=concentration not absorption; safety-homograph null=headline; localization-not-classification; router=exploratory; corrected selectivity 722x/676x; demote multi-member grouping + C-track; retract the Georgia +0.561 near-noop win; note firing-signature != edit-handle (Bush/Cook clean signatures, max_kg 0.04/0.03 -> NO_MEANINGFUL); strip all iteration/rebuttal/infra scaffolding.

Confidence: HIGH on the Chanin diagnostic facts, all seven new cite IDs/authors, Arad's EMNLP 2025 venue, and the ICLR-2026 area fit (all directly grep-verified). MEDIUM on the CorrSteer venue (genuine arXiv-Comments-vs-iclr.cc conflict -> cited as preprint + flagged). The two dependency research_out.json files were not on disk; their settled content was reconstructed from the plan's STEP 0 enumeration and the executor's matching project memory, and every NEW claim was independently verified, so the locked-cite carry is reliable but should be cross-checked against the actual dependency files if they become available.

sources.title: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders (Chanin et al.)
sources.summary: Confirms the absorption diagnostic is fully SUPERVISED and spelling-bound: LR probe on ground-truth first-letter labels -> SAE latent with max encoder-cosine to the probe = parent; ablation on the first-letter logit + probe-projection test identifies the absorber (e.g. latent 1085 absorbs 'starts-with-S' 6510 on _short); absorption_rate = num_absorptions / lr_probe_true_positives with cosine>0.025 and 1.0 ablation-gap thresholds. Demonstrations are entirely the first-letter task. Grounds D1.

sources.title: OpenReview meta-review: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in SAEs
sources.summary: Reviewer/meta note that 'the feature absorption metric and the empirical results focus on the first-letter task, and the generalizability to other tasks would be good to have' -- corroborates that Chanin's diagnostic is form-bound to spelling (supports D1 delta).

sources.title: SAEs Are Good for Steering -- If You Select the Right Features (Arad, Mueller, Belinkov)
sources.summary: EMNLP 2025 main (DOI 10.18653/v1/2025.emnlp-main.519; arXiv 2505.20063). Input vs OUTPUT scores; filtering out low-output-score features yields 2-3x steering improvement, competitive with supervised methods; high input/output scores rarely co-occur. Headline D2 cite: which precise feature you select drives steering quality, not the mechanism. Also notes SAEs otherwise underperform DiffMean/optimization baselines -> supports localization-not-classification framing.

sources.title: CorrSteer: Generation-Time LLM Steering via Correlated SAE Features (Cho, Wu, Koshiyama)
sources.summary: Correlation-based feature selection extracts 'more relevant features, thereby reducing spurious correlations' -> specificity reduces collateral (D2). arXiv Comments (v3, May 2026): '45 pages... Accepted at ICML 2026' -- but also listed at iclr.cc/virtual/2026; cite as preprint + venue flag.

sources.title: Interpretable Steering of LLMs with Feature Guided Activation Additions (FGAA; Soo et al.)
sources.summary: Density filtering removes features exceeding an activation-density threshold so only specialized (low-density, high-precision) features are used; relevance + concreteness filtering. Supports the concentration axis (sparse firing + high per-sub-context precision = clean handle). Preprint (no venue in Comments).

sources.title: Steering Large Language Model Activations in Sparse Spaces (SAS; Bayat, Rahimi-Kalahroudi, Pezeshki, Chandar, Vincent)
sources.summary: Scaling SAE width increases monosemanticity/sparsity of steering vectors, 'better disentangling features by reducing overlap' for finer-grained, more reliable interventions -> sparser/more-concentrated = sharper gate (D2). Preprint (OpenReview VGw1viYliK).

sources.title: Pre-Intervention Prediction of Sparse Autoencoder Steering Side Effects (Duan)
sources.summary: Strongest direct D2 support: a pre-intervention screening framework FORECASTS SAE steering side effects (effect stability + collateral spread) from feature statistics computed BEFORE steering, on GPT-2-small/Pythia-70M/Gemma-2-2B. Makes 'concentration/feature-statistics predict collateral' an externally tested claim. Preprint (Jun 2026).

sources.title: Improving Steering Vectors by Targeting Sparse Autoencoder Features (SAE-TS; Chalnev, Siu, Conmy)
sources.summary: SAE-Targeted Steering finds steering vectors to target SPECIFIC SAE features while MINIMIZING unintended side effects; balances steering effect with coherence better than CAA and SAE feature steering. Supporting D2 cite. Preprint.

sources.title: Scaling Monosemanticity: Extracting Interpretable Features from Claude 3 Sonnet (Templeton et al., Anthropic 2024)
sources.summary: Operationalizes SPECIFICITY as 'when the feature is active, the relevant concept is reliably present in the context' and influence-on-behavior; larger SAEs produce features that activate more selectively. Grounds the conditional-gate-sharpness intuition (a precise feature's firing IS a sharp gate). Cite by URL (no arXiv canonical).

sources.title: ICLR 2026 Call for Papers
sources.summary: Lists solicited topics including 'societal considerations including fairness, safety, privacy' and 'visualization or interpretation of learned representations' and 'datasets and benchmarks' -> clean area fit for the localization + editable-feature-KG framing. ICLR primary per goal (double-blind).

sources.title: ICML 2026 Dates and Deadlines
sources.summary: Main-conference full-paper deadline Jan 28 2026 (abstract Jan 23) -> the live cycle for a mid-2026 paper is ICLR 2026; ICML 2026 is an acceptable AREA fallback. Confirms venue timing for D3.

sources.title: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in SAEs -- NeurIPS 2025 poster
sources.summary: Confirms the locked venue for Chanin et al. 2409.14507 = NeurIPS 2025 (carried verbatim in the locked-cite table).

sources.title: Toy Models of Feature Absorption in SAEs (LessWrong)
sources.summary: Defines absorption as 'gerrymandered SAE latents which appear to track an interpretable concept but have holes in their recall' -- the recall-hole concept CCRG anchors on. Defensive novelty check: no existing method uses the recall hole as the selection anchor.

sources.title: CorrSteer listing on iclr.cc/virtual/2026
sources.summary: CorrSteer also appears on the ICLR 2026 virtual site, conflicting with the arXiv Comments 'Accepted at ICML 2026'. Basis for the unresolved venue flag -> cite as preprint and verify at camera-ready.

sources.title: Arad et al. summary (SAEs underperform DiffMean / optimization for steering)
sources.summary: Independent restatement that SAEs underperform DiffMean- and optimization-based methods for steering tasks before feature selection -- corroborates the goal's 'simple baselines often outperform raw-latent SAE methods' and the localization-not-classification reframe.

follow_up_questions: Did the executed iter-8 M3''' max-precision ablation show set-cover BEATS or TIES the single max-precision selector (i.e. is n_adds_value>0)? If a TIE, lock the 'anchored recall-hole-guided precision selection' identity and demote (1-1/e) to motivation; if not, retain set-cover for the k>1 cases only.

Did the executed M2''' expanded base land >=4 independent concentrated edit wins under the FAIR bounded-beta d_sub-gated control (-> OUTCOME-A) or stay thin / get matched by the fair control (-> OUTCOME-B, expected per BEATS=0/FAIR_CLOSES=4/n_concentrated_wins=0)?

Is CorrSteer (2508.12535) actually accepted at ICML 2026 or ICLR 2026 (arXiv Comments vs iclr.cc/virtual conflict), and are the Karvonen-Marks workshop, DeBiasLens CVPR2026, and Deng ACL2026 dual-listing resolvable before camera-ready -- or should all four be cited as preprints?
